metadata.scope: La Grange / Fayette County TX Mattingly + Teichmueller + kin 1850-1950

born.source: TX Death Certificate #48257; Find A Grave #57057880

died.place: La Grange, Fayette County, Texas, USA

mother.name: Elizabeth Christie Mattingly
mother.source: TX Death Certificate #48257; Find A Grave #172173392

siblings.name: Sarah Allie Embree Poindexter
siblings.burial: Pie Flat Cemetery, Roger Mills County OK
siblings.note: Half-sibling — child of Elizabeth's first marriage to Martin L. Embree

siblings.name: Noble N. Mattingly
siblings.note: Settled Concho County TX, not Fayette County

spouse_parents.father: Charles H. Schroeder (1858-1932), buried La Grange Cemetery
spouse_parents.mother: Delphine Schroeder (1867-1929), buried La Grange Cemetery

children.name: Dr. Claude Mattingly
children.note: TX Death Cert 4918; suicide in Austin; Marine Corps WWI veteran; UT Medical Branch Galveston graduate

children.name: Leroy Teichmueller Mattingly Sr.
children.burial: Mission Burial Park South, San Antonio, Section 4 Restland
children.note: TX Death Cert #68070; address at death: 261 Pennsylvania Ave, Braunfels TX. Birth year was previously recorded as 1898 in family files — CORRECTED to 1896 per death certificate.

children.name: Edward Mattingly Jr.
children.church: St. James Episcopal Church, La Grange (served as Senior Warden)
children.occupation: Architect (Texas A&M B.Arch 1936); WWII Colonel (Eisenhower's staff SHAEF)
military_awards: Belgium Croix de Guerre with palm
children.community: Established Fayette County Animal Shelter; TX Child Care Advisory Board

children.name: Mamie Mattingly
children.note: Oral tradition; not confirmed as distinct FAG memorial in Fayette County; possibly died infant ~1900

occupations.title: Assistant Cashier
occupations.employer: First National Bank of La Grange
occupations.source: Lotto 1902 p.326 bank advertisement; TSHA Hans Teichmueller entry

lotto_1902_exact_quotes.text: Mrs. May Mattingly, wife of the assistant cashier of the First National Bank, died two years ago
lotto_1902_exact_quotes.context: Hans Teichmueller biography, describing his daughter May
First National Bank of La Grange advertisement

lotto_1902_exact_quotes.text: The following gentlemen constitute the present city government: Robert Sample, mayor; Will Loessin, marshal; Ed. Mattingly, treasurer; Frank Rosenthal, secretary; E. H. Moss, attorney.

parents.father: August Teichmüller, officer in Brunswick army (died before 1856)

persons.occupation: District Judge, 22nd Judicial District, 1884-1901 (three terms); lawyer (bar admitted May 16 1866); county judge 1869-70
persons.note: Court adjourned out of respect on death Jan 1901. Gov. Sayers appointed L. H. Moore as replacement.

children.name: Minette (Minnetta) Teichmueller Pohl
children.note: Muralist; WPA Federal Art Project; 'The Law, Texas Rangers' mural at Smithville TX post office; married E.R. Richardson 1913-01-22, later Hugo Pohl; lived to 99

children.name: Anna Elizabeth Teichmueller
children.note: Never married; lived with sister Minette in San Antonio after 1920

children.name: Fifth Teichmueller child
children.note: Lotto 1902 states 'five children total'; only four are named in accessible sources

persons.note: Ed's death certificate names 'a Thomas Mattingly' as father. Full identity, birth/death dates, burial, and parents are UNVERIFIED in accessible free sources.
persons.wife: Elizabeth Christie (1833 KY - 1876 MO; Elizabeth's prior marriage to Martin L. Embree produced Sarah Allie Embree)
persons.location: Missouri — likely Daviess County or Gentry County, NW Missouri

persons.display_name: Blanche Schroeder Mattingly (Ed Sr.'s second wife)

parents.father: Charles H. Schroeder (1858-1932), La Grange Cemetery

persons.note: Settled Concho County TX (Millersview), separate from La Grange/Fayette County branch

la_grange_1902.composition: American, German, Bohemian; approximately one-third African-American
lodges: Fayette County German Mutual Aid Society

st_james_episcopal.name: St. James Episcopal Church, La Grange
st_james_episcopal.mattingly_connection: Edward Mattingly Jr. served as Senior Warden

churches_la_grange_fayette.catholic: Heavy presence; Czech and German immigrant communities; St. Joseph's, St. John's
churches_la_grange_fayette.lutheran: Major German/Wendish denomination; county had 60 churches by 1990s with Lutheran+Catholic = half
churches_la_grange_fayette.note: By 1990s Fayette County had 60 churches; Lutheran and Catholic = half the total. Protestant (Methodist 1838, Baptist 1839, Presbyterian 1841, Episcopal 1852) from earliest settlement.
churches_la_grange_fayette.source: TSHA Fayette County; Lotto 1902; Edward Mattingly Jr. obituary

german_czech_settlement.biegel_settlement: 1832, first German community in Fayette County
german_czech_settlement.dubina: 1856, first Bohemian settlement in Texas, in Fayette County
german_czech_settlement.1880s_wave: Major second wave of German and Bohemian immigration
german_czech_settlement.1890_foreign_born: ~25% of county (7,856 of 31,481 residents); Germany 3,667; Austria-Hungary 3,224
